# Codex BRIEF gate — I-perm-022 (#1214): verifier cited-span normalization

HARD ITERATION CAP: 5 per document. This is iter 2 of 5.

## ITER-2 CHANGES (addressing your iter-1 P1s + P2)

- **iter-1 P1 (zero-width → space could ADD support, §-1.1 LETHAL) — FIXED.** Zero-width
  FORMAT controls (ZWSP U+200B, ZWNJ U+200C, ZWJ U+200D, BOM/ZWNBSP U+FEFF) are now
  DELETED (joined), NEVER spaced. Spacing could split a negation ("in<ZWJ>effective" ->
  "in effective", "un<ZWSP>safe" -> "un safe") and fabricate apparent support; deleting can
  only make a span LESS matchable (a missed recovery), never ADD support. Only TRUE
  no-break spaces (NBSP U+00A0, figure space U+2007, narrow NBSP U+202F) become a space.
  New adversarial test `test_zero_width_is_DELETED_not_spaced_negation_safe` proves
  "in<ZWJ>effective" -> "ineffective" and "the drug was in<ZWSP>effective" yields
  "ineffective" (NOT "in effective"). Behavioral run confirms all 10 cases incl.
  negation-safety + zero-digit invariants.
- **iter-1 P1 (flag inert on the paid path) — FIXED.** `PG_GATE_B_SPAN_NORMALIZE` is now in
  `_FULL_CAPABILITY_BENCHMARK_SLATE` (="1"), `_BENCHMARK_FORCE_ON_FLAGS` (force-set, no
  setdefault drift), AND `_BENCHMARK_PREFLIGHT_REQUIRED_FLAGS` (fail-closed if not active on
  a paid run) — same triple as the FX-03 cited-span precedent.
- **iter-1 P2 (literal invisible chars in the regex vs \u escapes) — disclosed.** The
  `_LIGATURE_MAP` / `_ZERO_WIDTH_RE` / `_NBSP_SPACE_RE` character classes currently hold the
  literal codepoints (behaviorally verified correct by the test suite + an ascii-safe
  codepoint probe). Editor tooling kept mangling typed `\u` escapes; the classes are
  correct and tested. Flag if you want this blocked on a \u-escape rewrite (cosmetic
  maintainability, not behavior).
- **Build:** 8 span-normalize tests + 8 existing FX-03 cited-span tests pass (flag-off
  byte-identical integration confirmed).

## Issue (#1214, I-perm-022)
Un-normalized PDF-extraction artifacts (ligatures, line-break hyphenation, NBSP/zero-width)
in the cited SPAN reach the four-role second evaluators (Sentinel decomposition + Judge)
verbatim and can flip a genuinely-supported atom to "unsupported" → surfaced as
[confidence:low]. Normalize the cited span BEFORE the 2nd evaluator; recover TRUE positives
only; gate NEVER loosened. DoD: default-OFF flag, byte-identical when off; faithfulness gates
NEVER relaxed; unit tests; Codex diff-gate APPROVE; paid §-1.1 smoke; wired into Gate-B slate.

The full grounded forensic is at `.codex/I-perm-022/forensic.md` (read it).

## Design (as built)
NORMALIZE THE SPAN ONLY (artifacts are source-side); grade the CLAIM as-authored.
- `_normalize_span_text(text)` in `src/polaris_graph/roles/native_gate_b_inputs.py`, gated by
  default-OFF `PG_GATE_B_SPAN_NORMALIZE` (OFF → returns input unchanged → byte-identical):
  1. Ligatures U+FB00..U+FB06 → ASCII (the unambiguous, high-value repair).
  2. Line-break (soft) hyphenation, ALPHABETIC neighbours ONLY: `([A-Za-z])-[ \t]*\n[ \t]*([A-Za-z])`.
  3. Invisible/zero-width/NBSP → space; collapse space/tab runs.
  - ZERO digit modification — every rule matches ALPHABETIC context only, so numbers, ranges
    ("20-\n30"), and negatives ("-1.07") are byte-preserved (the I-gen-005 lesson).
- Wired at `_resolve_evidence` (the EvidenceDocument the evaluator reads):
  `text=_normalize_span_text(_cited_window_text(text, token))`. Records keep FULL un-normalized
  text. Slate force-on `PG_GATE_B_SPAN_NORMALIZE=1`.

## Faithfulness analysis (the crux you must verify)
1. **Repair, not rewrite — recovers TPs only.** The normalizer maps a garbled token to its
   true form and ADDS NO content. If the span genuinely does not state a claim's atom, the
   repaired span STILL does not → the atom stays unsupported → the claim stays non-VERIFIED. A
   genuine negative cannot flip, because no new support is introduced.
2. **Zero digit modification (testable invariant).** Every regex matches alphabetic context
   only; ligature codepoints carry no digits; de-hyphenation is `[A-Za-z]-...-[A-Za-z]`.
   `test_numeric_difference_does_NOT_collapse` proves `2%`/`20%` stay distinct and a digit
   line-break hyphen is preserved.
3. **Gate untouched.** strict_verify, D8, `_compose_final_verdict` fail-closed, Mirror doc_id
   binding, Sentinel UNGROUNDED override, the VERIFIED coverage credit are all unchanged. The
   only change is the TEXT the evaluator reads, made faithfully comparable to its own source.
4. **No recovery target.** The fix accepts whatever recovers (possibly far fewer than the
   drb_76 "12"); it is NOT tuned to a count (that would force-pass genuine negatives).

## Honesty caveats (from the forensic — weigh these)
- The drb_76 "12 false negatives" is NOT confirmed artifact-driven: in the captured run the one
  artifact-bearing row (ev_206) feeds no Sentinel-unsupported claim, and MOST of the 8 are
  GENUINE semantic negatives that MUST stay negative. The fix is defensible prophylaxis + one
  demonstrated truncation case (05-007 "natural kill[er] cell"), proven per-claim only by the
  paid §-1.1 smoke.
- De-hyphenation is irreducibly imperfect at the seam ("oxaliplatin-\ninduced" residual);
  ligatures are the clean win, hyphenation is best-effort/alphabetic-only/documented. A wrong
  join only FAILS to match (a missed recovery), never ADDS support.
- Truncation artifacts ("natural kill" vs "killer") are NOT fixed here (need fuzzy/edit-distance
  = different + riskier; out of scope). Persistent cross-run verdict cache deferred (the in-run
  `_dedup_key` already inherits the normalized identity for free since we normalize upstream).

## Tests (`tests/polaris_graph/test_span_normalize_iperm022.py`, 7) + FX-03 regression
flag-off byte-identical; ligatures repaired; alphabetic hyphen joined (+ documented residual);
**numeric difference does NOT collapse**; NBSP/ZWSP/BOM → space + run collapse; genuine negative
gains no content; empty-safe. 7 new pass; 8 existing FX-03 cited-span tests pass (flag-off
integration → byte-identical EvidenceDocument.text).

## Questions
1. Is "normalize span / grade claim as-authored, recovers-TP-only, zero-digit-mod" sufficient to
   keep this faithfulness-NEUTRAL? Any path where a repaired span ADDS support to a genuine
   negative?
2. Is deferring the persistent cross-run verdict cache (keeping only the free in-run dedup
   identity) acceptable to stay under the 200-LOC cap?
3. Is folding the per-claim causal proof into the paid §-1.1 smoke (vs claiming it offline)
   the honest call, given the "12 FN" is not artifact-confirmed?

.codex\forensic\drb_90_adas_liability_stderr.txt:10837:outputs\audit_2026_05_18\all_issues.json:1:[{"body":"## Problem\n\nClaude repeatedly ends a turn with a prose status/next-step report instead of\nthe next tool call, after finishing a unit of work (a PR merge, a grounded\nscan, \"the next step is X\"). The user has flagged this 5+ times\n(2026-05-07 → 2026-05-18: \"why you pause here? any valid reason? figure out a\nway to resolve this constant pausing behaviour\").\n\nEvery prior fix was a *rule* (CLAUDE.md §8.1 banned STATUS blocks; §8.2 banned\nprose-between-merge-and-branch; §8.3.10 banned named rationalizations) — and\neach failed because the pause is a decision made at stop-time and the agent\nrationalizes a fresh exception. Per `failure_28_commits`: \"promises don't\nwork; structural removal does.\" A rule the agent must remember and apply is\nnot structural. The only structural mechanism is one **external to the agent's\nin-the-moment judgment** — a Stop hook.\n\n## Proposed mechanism\n\nA Claude Code `Stop` hook (`.claude/hooks/`, project-scoped per\n`stop_hook_must_be_project_scoped`) that, when the agent attempts to end a\nturn, decides allow-vs-block:\n\n- **Allow the stop** if any of: the last message is a user turn awaiting a\n  reply; a `state/halt_*` marker exists; the transcript shows a Codex iter-5\n  REQUEST_CHANGES; `state/active_issue.json` shows no in-progress work.\n- **Block the stop** (return the `block` decision + a reason) if there is\n  in-progress issue-queue work and none of the allow conditions hold — inject\n  \"continue: the next output is a tool call advancing the issue, not prose.\"\n\n## Hard constraints (this area is delicate)\n\n- **Loop-safety is mandatory.** A Stop hook that always blocks creates an\n  infinite loop. It must bound the number of consecutive blocks and/or only\n  block when the transcript shows a *just-completed* sub-step. See\n  `feedback_no_unbounded_polling_loops`.\n- Per #524 (I-pin-001): the prior `stop_hook_v3.py` canonical-pin check was\n  **deliberately neutralized** for false-firing on Windows CRLF. This hook\n  must not repeat that — no false blocks; when uncertain, allow.\n- Must distinguish \"user just asked a question\" (allow) from \"agent finished a\n  PR and the queue has more\" (block) — the core discriminator.\n- Codex APPROVE on brief + diff; offline test of both the allow and block\n  paths before merge.\n\n## Acceptance\n\n- A `Stop` hook is wired in `.claude/settings.json`, project-scoped, that\n  blocks a turn-end during incomplete issue-queue work with no fired halt.\n- Loop-safety: bounded consecutive blocks; a documented test of the\n  infinite-loop guard.\n- The allow path (user question / halt marker / no active issue) is tested.\n- Does not re-introduce the CRLF false-fire class of bug.\n\nRefs: CLAUDE.md §8.1/§8.2/§8.3.10; `feedback_dont_pause_keep_executing`;\n`failure_28_commits`; #524 (prior stop-hook neutralization).","closedAt":null,"createdAt":"2026-05-18T07:28:59Z","labels":[],"number":589,"state":"OPEN","title":"I-ci-002 — Stop hook to block fake-pause turn-ends during incomplete issue-queue work"},{"body":"## Bug\n\nSurfaced by the #400 (I-beat-001) BEAT-BOTH §-1.1 line-by-line audit — `outputs/audits/I-beat-001/cross_review_v12.md`, claim Q3-C1.\n\nIn the POLARIS Q3 (workforce / gen-AI white-collar employment) report, the gen-AI occupational-exposure decimals **75.5% / 68.4% / 62.6%** are cited to **Goldman Sachs 2023**. The decimals do not match the Goldman Sachs 2023 baseline — they match the **PWBM 2025** (Penn Wharton Budget Model, Eloundou/BLS-derived) exposure table.\n\nDecimals are correct (PWBM published them); the **source attribution is wrong** (citation points at Goldman Sachs 2023).\n\n## Classification\n\nThis is **NOT a fabrication** — the numbers are real and traceable. It is a citation-attribution error caught by the line-by-line audit (Codex independently traced the decimals to PWBM 2025 during cross-review iter14).\n\n## Likely root cause\n\nThe `workforce` scope template / domain backend, or the generator's source-binding for the exposure-statistics claim, attributes the exposure table to the wrong retrieved source. A\ndetached fetch-backend task whose Playwright cleanup (`_try_crawl4ai` →\n`_safe_close_crawler` → `__aexit__`) is genuinely wedged would therefore\ndelay — in the theoretical worst case, indefinitely — `asyncio.run`\n**teardown**, *after* all artifacts and the manifest are already written.\n\nIn practice Playwright browser cleanup is slow-worst-case, not\ninfinite-worst-case (CDP has internal timeouts; the browser subprocess is\nSIGTERM'd by Playwright's own process management), so the detached task is\nexpected to die within a bounded time. Observed in the I-gen-003 V4 Pro smoke: a single section-outline call produced `content=1791 bytes` + **`reasoning=17521 bytes`** (4790 reasoning tokens vs 5388 output tokens). The reasoning trace is roughly 3× the size of the final content.\n\n**Right now POLARIS discards the reasoning trace entirely.** `multi_section_generator._call_section` (and the outline/repair call sites) return `(response.content, input_tokens, output_tokens)` — `response.reasoning` is dropped on the floor. `LLMResponse` carries a `.reasoning` field but nothing in the generator or audit_ir persists it. A reviewer auditing a POLARIS report cannot see how the model reasoned its way to the cited prose.\n\nFor an audit-grade clinical tool this is a transparency hole: the reasoning IS evidence of process. It must be captured, stored SEPARATELY from the output content (never mixed into report.md / verified_text), and surfaced in the audit bundle.\n\n## Acceptance criteria\n\n1. **Capture** — `_call_section`, the outline call, and the I-bug-108 sentence-repair call each return / thread through `response.reasoning` alongside `content`. `SectionResult` (and the outline result) gain a `reasoning_trace` field.\n2. **Store separately** — per run, write a `reasoning_trace.jsonl` (or per-section files) into the run output dir, ONE record per LLM call: `{section, call_type (outline|section|repair|regen), attempt_n, model, reasoning_text, content_text, input_tokens, output_tokens, reasoning_tokens, timestamp}`. The reasoning text is NEVER merged into `report.md`, `verified_text`, or the verified-sentence stream — those stay content-only.\n3. **Manifest + bundle** — `manifest.json` references the reasoning trace file(s); the audit bundle (`bundle.tar.gz`) includes them so an external reviewer gets the reasoning alongside the report + provenance.\n4. **Transparency surface** — `/transparency` (or the bundle README) documents that the reasoning trace is captured + where it lives + that it is model-process evidence, not verified claims. (Frontend Inspector rendering of the trace MAY be a follow-up — the core requirement here is capture + separate storage + bundle inclusion.)\n5. **Separation invariant** — a test asserts the reasoning text never appears in `report.md` / verified prose, and that `strict_verify` is never run against reasoning text. Reasoning is process-transparency, NOT a claim source.\n6. **Works for all models** — for non-reasoning-first models `response.reasoning` is usually empty/None; the trace record still gets written (empty reasoning_text) so the artifact schema is uniform. No crash, no-op when reasoning absent.\n7. Codex diff review APPROVE.\n\n## Depends on\n\n- I-gen-003 (GH#495) — V4 Pro must actually run as the generator first. This Issue lands immediately after.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Frontend Inspector UI for browsing the reasoning trace — separate follow-up (the bundle inclusion + separate storage is the P0 transparency requirement; the UI is P1 polish).\n- Retroactively capturing reasoning for past runs.\n\n## Severity\n\nP0 for the Carney demo transparency story — POLARIS is positioned on auditable per-sentence faithfulness; \"we throw away the model's reasoning\" is a hole a sophisticated reviewer (or Carney's office) would find immediately.\n\n🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)","closedAt":"2026-05-17T10:49:08Z","createdAt":"2026-05-14T16:17:01Z","labels":[],"number":496,"state":"CLOSED","title":"I-gen-004 — capture + store V4 Pro reasoning trace separately from output content, transparently"},{"body":"## Context\n\nOperator directive 2026-05-14: POLARIS must use **DeepSeek V4 Pro** as the generator — it is the model named in the OVH H200 procurement spec, `docs/transparency.md`, `docs/carney_demo_runbook.md`, and the whole sovereignty narrative. Edges are: (a) sentence→source from provenance tokens `[#ev:source_id:start-end]`, (b) source↔source co-citation when 2 sources support same claim, (c) source↔source contradiction (from F8 contradiction matrix), (d) frame↔sentence section membership.\n- **Must integrate with existing surfaces**: F5 Inspector Sheet (click handler), F6 EvidenceTooltip (hover), F8 contradiction badges (red edges), F13 pin replay (diff overlay), F15 audit bundle (static SVG export for print).\n- **Stack**: Next.js 16 + React 19 + shadcn/ui + Tailwind v4 + TypeScript strict.\n- **Performance target**: 200-sentence report × 100 sources × ~600 edges should render in <500ms first paint, hover-latency <100ms.\n- **Print/standalone export**: must produce a static SVG/PNG for audit bundle (a frozen snapshot the third-party reviewer can verify offline).\n- **Accessibility**: keyboard navigation, screen-reader labels, WCAG-AA.\n- **License**: MIT or compatible (no GPL).\n\n## Candidates to evaluate\n\n1. **Reagraph** — WebGL React-native, MIT, clustering built-in\n2. **react-force-graph** (vasturiano) — 2D/3D/VR/AR, MIT, mature\n3. **Sigma.js + react-sigma + graphology + ForceAtlas2** — most performant for large graphs\n4. **Cytoscape.js + react-cytoscapejs** — graph-theory-first (BFS, shortest-path); .codex\forensic\drb_78_parkinsons_dbs_stderr.txt:8093:.codex\forensic\drb_76_gut_microbiota_crc_stderr.txt:319:outputs\audit_2026_05_18\all_issues.json:1:[{"body":"## Problem\n\nClaude repeatedly ends a turn with a prose status/next-step report instead of\nthe next tool call, after finishing a unit of work (a PR merge, a grounded\nscan, \"the next step is X\"). The user has flagged this 5+ times\n(2026-05-07 → 2026-05-18: \"why you pause here? any valid reason? figure out a\nway to resolve this constant pausing behaviour\").\n\nEvery prior fix was a *rule* (CLAUDE.md §8.1 banned STATUS blocks; §8.2 banned\nprose-between-merge-and-branch; §8.3.10 banned named rationalizations) — and\neach failed because the pause is a decision made at stop-time and the agent\nrationalizes a fresh exception. Per `failure_28_commits`: \"promises don't\nwork; structural removal does.\" A rule the agent must remember and apply is\nnot structural. The only structural mechanism is one **external to the agent's\nin-the-moment judgment** — a Stop hook.\n\n## Proposed mechanism\n\nA Claude Code `Stop` hook (`.claude/hooks/`, project-scoped per\n`stop_hook_must_be_project_scoped`) that, when the agent attempts to end a\nturn, decides allow-vs-block:\n\n- **Allow the stop** if any of: the last message is a user turn awaiting a\n  reply; a `state/halt_*` marker exists; the transcript shows a Codex iter-5\n  REQUEST_CHANGES; `state/active_issue.json` shows no in-progress work.\n- **Block the stop** (return the `block` decision + a reason) if there is\n  in-progress issue-queue work and none of the allow conditions hold — inject\n  \"continue: the next output is a tool call advancing the issue, not prose.\"\n\n## Hard constraints (this area is delicate)\n\n- **Loop-safety is mandatory.** A Stop hook that always blocks creates an\n  infinite loop. It must bound the number of consecutive blocks and/or only\n  block when the transcript shows a *just-completed* sub-step. See\n  `feedback_no_unbounded_polling_loops`.\n- Per #524 (I-pin-001): the prior `stop_hook_v3.py` canonical-pin check was\n  **deliberately neutralized** for false-firing on Windows CRLF. This hook\n  must not repeat that — no false blocks; when uncertain, allow.\n- Must distinguish \"user just asked a question\" (allow) from \"agent finished a\n  PR and the queue has more\" (block) — the core discriminator.\n- Codex APPROVE on brief + diff; offline test of both the allow and block\n  paths before merge.\n\n## Acceptance\n\n- A `Stop` hook is wired in `.claude/settings.json`, project-scoped, that\n  blocks a turn-end during incomplete issue-queue work with no fired halt.\n- Loop-safety: bounded consecutive blocks; a documented test of the\n  infinite-loop guard.\n- The allow path (user question / halt marker / no active issue) is tested.\n- Does not re-introduce the CRLF false-fire class of bug.\n\nRefs: CLAUDE.md §8.1/§8.2/§8.3.10; `feedback_dont_pause_keep_executing`;\n`failure_28_commits`; #524 (prior stop-hook neutralization).","closedAt":null,"createdAt":"2026-05-18T07:28:59Z","labels":[],"number":589,"state":"OPEN","title":"I-ci-002 — Stop hook to block fake-pause turn-ends during incomplete issue-queue work"},{"body":"## Bug\n\nSurfaced by the #400 (I-beat-001) BEAT-BOTH §-1.1 line-by-line audit — `outputs/audits/I-beat-001/cross_review_v12.md`, claim Q3-C1.\n\nIn the POLARIS Q3 (workforce / gen-AI white-collar employment) report, the gen-AI occupational-exposure decimals **75.5% / 68.4% / 62.6%** are cited to **Goldman Sachs 2023**. The decimals do not match the Goldman Sachs 2023 baseline — they match the **PWBM 2025** (Penn Wharton Budget Model, Eloundou/BLS-derived) exposure table.\n\nDecimals are correct (PWBM published them); the **source attribution is wrong** (citation points at Goldman Sachs 2023).\n\n## Classification\n\nThis is **NOT a fabrication** — the numbers are real and traceable. Two issue-ID classes in the current queue do\nnot survive this:\n\n### 1. `-followup` issues collapse onto their parent\n\nIssues `I-rdy-019-followup` (#558), `I-rdy-010-followup` (#537),\n`I-rdy-011-followup` (#539), `I-rdy-016-followup` (#547),\n`I-rdy-017-followup` (#549), `I-gen-004-followup` (#561) — a branch\n`bot/I-rdy-019-followup-*` canonicalizes to `ISSUE_ID = I-rdy-019`, i.e. the\n**parent issue's** (#515's) already-merged `.codex/I-rdy-019/` directory.\nThe gate then reads the parent's `codex_diff.patch` and excludes the wrong\ndir from the canonical-diff computation.\n\n**Observed on #558 / PR #569:** `codex-required` failed —\n`declared (trailer): 17dd8d74…` (read from `.codex/I-rdy-019/codex_diff.patch`,\n#515's file) vs `actual: 63ccfc8f…` (canonical diff that did NOT exclude\n`.codex/I-rdy-019-followup/**`). A\ndetached fetch-backend task whose Playwright cleanup (`_try_crawl4ai` →\n`_safe_close_crawler` → `__aexit__`) is genuinely wedged would therefore\ndelay — in the theoretical worst case, indefinitely — `asyncio.run`\n**teardown**, *after* all artifacts and the manifest are already written.\n\nIn practice Playwright browser cleanup is slow-worst-case, not\ninfinite-worst-case (CDP has internal timeouts; the browser subprocess is\nSIGTERM'd by Playwright's own process management), so the detached task is\nexpected to die within a bounded time. Observed in the I-gen-003 V4 Pro smoke: a single section-outline call produced `content=1791 bytes` + **`reasoning=17521 bytes`** (4790 reasoning tokens vs 5388 output tokens). The reasoning trace is roughly 3× the size of the final content.\n\n**Right now POLARIS discards the reasoning trace entirely.** `multi_section_generator._call_section` (and the outline/repair call sites) return `(response.content, input_tokens, output_tokens)` — `response.reasoning` is dropped on the floor. `LLMResponse` carries a `.reasoning` field but nothing in the generator or audit_ir persists it. A reviewer auditing a POLARIS report cannot see how the model reasoned its way to the cited prose.\n\nFor an audit-grade clinical tool this is a transparency hole: the reasoning IS evidence of process. It must be captured, stored SEPARATELY from the output content (never mixed into report.md / verified_text), and surfaced in the audit bundle.\n\n## Acceptance criteria\n\n1. **Capture** — `_call_section`, the outline call, and the I-bug-108 sentence-repair call each return / thread through `response.reasoning` alongside `content`. `SectionResult` (and the outline result) gain a `reasoning_trace` field.\n2. **Store separately** — per run, write a `reasoning_trace.jsonl` (or per-section files) into the run output dir, ONE record per LLM call: `{section, call_type (outline|section|repair|regen), attempt_n, model, reasoning_text, content_text, input_tokens, output_tokens, reasoning_tokens, timestamp}`. The reasoning text is NEVER merged into `report.md`, `verified_text`, or the verified-sentence stream — those stay content-only.\n3. **Manifest + bundle** — `manifest.json` references the reasoning trace file(s); the audit bundle (`bundle.tar.gz`) includes them so an external reviewer gets the reasoning alongside the report + provenance.\n4. **Transparency surface** — `/transparency` (or the bundle README) documents that the reasoning trace is captured + where it lives + that it is model-process evidence, not verified claims. (Frontend Inspector rendering of the trace MAY be a follow-up — the core requirement here is capture + separate storage + bundle inclusion.)\n5. **Separation invariant** — a test asserts the reasoning text never appears in `report.md` / verified prose, and that `strict_verify` is never run against reasoning text. Reasoning is process-transparency, NOT a claim source.\n6. **Works for all models** — for non-reasoning-first models `response.reasoning` is usually empty/None; the trace record still gets written (empty reasoning_text) so the artifact schema is uniform. No crash, no-op when reasoning absent.\n7. Codex diff review APPROVE.\n\n## Depends on\n\n- I-gen-003 (GH#495) — V4 Pro must actually run as the generator first. This Issue lands immediately after.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Frontend Inspector UI for browsing the reasoning trace — separate follow-up (the bundle inclusion + separate storage is the P0 transparency requirement; the UI is P1 polish).\n- Retroactively capturing reasoning for past runs.\n\n## Severity\n\nP0 for the Carney demo transparency story — POLARIS is positioned on auditable per-sentence faithfulness; \"we throw away the model's reasoning\" is a hole a sophisticated reviewer (or Carney's office) would find immediately.\n\n🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)","closedAt":"2026-05-17T10:49:08Z","createdAt":"2026-05-14T16:17:01Z","labels":[],"number":496,"state":"CLOSED","title":"I-gen-004 — capture + store V4 Pro reasoning trace separately from output content, transparently"},{"body":"## Context\n\nOperator directive 2026-05-14: POLARIS must use **DeepSeek V4 Pro** as the generator — it is the model named in the OVH H200 procurement spec, `docs/transparency.md`, `docs/carney_demo_runbook.md`, and the whole sovereignty narrative. Right now the working generator is `deepseek-v3.2-exp` because of I-bug-091.\n\n## Why V4 Pro is currently parked (I-bug-091, 2026-05-09)\n\n`src/polaris_graph/llm/openrouter_client.py:255-271` reverted `PG_GENERATOR_MODEL` from V4 Pro to V3.2-Exp. With no upper bound, pip's resolver pulls latest-1.x of each package, hits the langchain 1.x ecosystem's internal version coupling, and backtracks through the entire release history trying to find a compatible set — effectively a hang.\n\nThis did not surface when I-carney-005 shipped Dockerfile.v6 because langchain 1.x had not been released / the resolver still converged then. .codex\forensic\drb_76_gut_microbiota_crc_stderr.txt:319:outputs\audit_2026_05_18\all_issues.json:1:[{"body":"## Problem\n\nClaude repeatedly ends a turn with a prose status/next-step report instead of\nthe next tool call, after finishing a unit of work (a PR merge, a grounded\nscan, \"the next step is X\"). The user has flagged this 5+ times\n(2026-05-07 → 2026-05-18: \"why you pause here? any valid reason? figure out a\nway to resolve this constant pausing behaviour\").\n\nEvery prior fix was a *rule* (CLAUDE.md §8.1 banned STATUS blocks; §8.2 banned\nprose-between-merge-and-branch; §8.3.10 banned named rationalizations) — and\neach failed because the pause is a decision made at stop-time and the agent\nrationalizes a fresh exception. Per `failure_28_commits`: \"promises don't\nwork; structural removal does.\" A rule the agent must remember and apply is\nnot structural. The only structural mechanism is one **external to the agent's\nin-the-moment judgment** — a Stop hook.\n\n## Proposed mechanism\n\nA Claude Code `Stop` hook (`.claude/hooks/`, project-scoped per\n`stop_hook_must_be_project_scoped`) that, when the agent attempts to end a\nturn, decides allow-vs-block:\n\n- **Allow the stop** if any of: the last message is a user turn awaiting a\n  reply; a `state/halt_*` marker exists; the transcript shows a Codex iter-5\n  REQUEST_CHANGES; `state/active_issue.json` shows no in-progress work.\n- **Block the stop** (return the `block` decision + a reason) if there is\n  in-progress issue-queue work and none of the allow conditions hold — inject\n  \"continue: the next output is a tool call advancing the issue, not prose.\"\n\n## Hard constraints (this area is delicate)\n\n- **Loop-safety is mandatory.** A Stop hook that always blocks creates an\n  infinite loop. It must bound the number of consecutive blocks and/or only\n  block when the transcript shows a *just-completed* sub-step. See\n  `feedback_no_unbounded_polling_loops`.\n- Per #524 (I-pin-001): the prior `stop_hook_v3.py` canonical-pin check was\n  **deliberately neutralized** for false-firing on Windows CRLF. This hook\n  must not repeat that — no false blocks; when uncertain, allow.\n- Must distinguish \"user just asked a question\" (allow) from \"agent finished a\n  PR and the queue has more\" (block) — the core discriminator.\n- Codex APPROVE on brief + diff; offline test of both the allow and block\n  paths before merge.\n\n## Acceptance\n\n- A `Stop` hook is wired in `.claude/settings.json`, project-scoped, that\n  blocks a turn-end during incomplete issue-queue work with no fired halt.\n- Loop-safety: bounded consecutive blocks; a documented test of the\n  infinite-loop guard.\n- The allow path (user question / halt marker / no active issue) is tested.\n- Does not re-introduce the CRLF false-fire class of bug.\n\nRefs: CLAUDE.md §8.1/§8.2/§8.3.10; `feedback_dont_pause_keep_executing`;\n`failure_28_commits`; #524 (prior stop-hook neutralization).","closedAt":null,"createdAt":"2026-05-18T07:28:59Z","labels":[],"number":589,"state":"OPEN","title":"I-ci-002 — Stop hook to block fake-pause turn-ends during incomplete issue-queue work"},{"body":"## Bug\n\nSurfaced by the #400 (I-beat-001) BEAT-BOTH §-1.1 line-by-line audit — `outputs/audits/I-beat-001/cross_review_v12.md`, claim Q3-C1.\n\nIn the POLARIS Q3 (workforce / gen-AI white-collar employment) report, the gen-AI occupational-exposure decimals **75.5% / 68.4% / 62.6%** are cited to **Goldman Sachs 2023**. A\ndetached fetch-backend task whose Playwright cleanup (`_try_crawl4ai` →\n`_safe_close_crawler` → `__aexit__`) is genuinely wedged would therefore\ndelay — in the theoretical worst case, indefinitely — `asyncio.run`\n**teardown**, *after* all artifacts and the manifest are already written.\n\nIn practice Playwright browser cleanup is slow-worst-case, not\ninfinite-worst-case (CDP has internal timeouts; the browser subprocess is\nSIGTERM'd by Playwright's own process management), so the detached task is\nexpected to die within a bounded time. Observed in the I-gen-003 V4 Pro smoke: a single section-outline call produced `content=1791 bytes` + **`reasoning=17521 bytes`** (4790 reasoning tokens vs 5388 output tokens). The reasoning trace is roughly 3× the size of the final content.\n\n**Right now POLARIS discards the reasoning trace entirely.** `multi_section_generator._call_section` (and the outline/repair call sites) return `(response.content, input_tokens, output_tokens)` — `response.reasoning` is dropped on the floor. `LLMResponse` carries a `.reasoning` field but nothing in the generator or audit_ir persists it. A reviewer auditing a POLARIS report cannot see how the model reasoned its way to the cited prose.\n\nFor an audit-grade clinical tool this is a transparency hole: the reasoning IS evidence of process. It must be captured, stored SEPARATELY from the output content (never mixed into report.md / verified_text), and surfaced in the audit bundle.\n\n## Acceptance criteria\n\n1. **Capture** — `_call_section`, the outline call, and the I-bug-108 sentence-repair call each return / thread through `response.reasoning` alongside `content`. `SectionResult` (and the outline result) gain a `reasoning_trace` field.\n2. **Store separately** — per run, write a `reasoning_trace.jsonl` (or per-section files) into the run output dir, ONE record per LLM call: `{section, call_type (outline|section|repair|regen), attempt_n, model, reasoning_text, content_text, input_tokens, output_tokens, reasoning_tokens, timestamp}`. The reasoning text is NEVER merged into `report.md`, `verified_text`, or the verified-sentence stream — those stay content-only.\n3. **Manifest + bundle** — `manifest.json` references the reasoning trace file(s); the audit bundle (`bundle.tar.gz`) includes them so an external reviewer gets the reasoning alongside the report + provenance.\n4. **Transparency surface** — `/transparency` (or the bundle README) documents that the reasoning trace is captured + where it lives + that it is model-process evidence, not verified claims. (Frontend Inspector rendering of the trace MAY be a follow-up — the core requirement here is capture + separate storage + bundle inclusion.)\n5. **Separation invariant** — a test asserts the reasoning text never appears in `report.md` / verified prose, and that `strict_verify` is never run against reasoning text. Reasoning is process-transparency, NOT a claim source.\n6. **Works for all models** — for non-reasoning-first models `response.reasoning` is usually empty/None; the trace record still gets written (empty reasoning_text) so the artifact schema is uniform. No crash, no-op when reasoning absent.\n7. Codex diff review APPROVE.\n\n## Depends on\n\n- I-gen-003 (GH#495) — V4 Pro must actually run as the generator first. This Issue lands immediately after.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Frontend Inspector UI for browsing the reasoning trace — separate follow-up (the bundle inclusion + separate storage is the P0 transparency requirement; the UI is P1 polish).\n- Retroactively capturing reasoning for past runs.\n\n## Severity\n\nP0 for the Carney demo transparency story — POLARIS is positioned on auditable per-sentence faithfulness; \"we throw away the model's reasoning\" is a hole a sophisticated reviewer (or Carney's office) would find immediately.\n\n🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)","closedAt":"2026-05-17T10:49:08Z","createdAt":"2026-05-14T16:17:01Z","labels":[],"number":496,"state":"CLOSED","title":"I-gen-004 — capture + store V4 Pro reasoning trace separately from output content, transparently"},{"body":"## Context\n\nOperator directive 2026-05-14: POLARIS must use **DeepSeek V4 Pro** as the generator — it is the model named in the OVH H200 procurement spec, `docs/transparency.md`, `docs/carney_demo_runbook.md`, and the whole sovereignty narrative. Had to kill the build (runaway, §8.4).\n\n## Root cause\n\n`Dockerfile.v6:47-51` assembles the pipeline-A requirements by sed-stripping `requirements.txt`:\n\n```dockerfile\nCOPY requirements.txt requirements-v6.txt ./\nRUN sed -e '/^google-generativeai/d' [...protobuf strip...] \\\n        requirements.txt > /tmp/requirements-v6-pipeline-a.txt && \\\n    pip install --no-cache-dir -r /tmp/requirements-v6-pipeline-a.txt -r requirements-v6.txt\n```\n\nThe langchain pins in `requirements.txt:35-40` are **unbounded `>=`**:\n\n```\nlangchain>=0.3.0\nlanggraph>=0.2.0\nlangchain-openai>=0.2.0\nlangchain-community>=0.3.0\nlangchain-core>=0.3.0\n```\n\nThese were written when langchain was 0.3.x. langchain has since released the 1.x line (build log shows `langchain-1.2.17`, `langchain-openai-1.1.16` available). With no upper bound, pip's resolver pulls latest-1.x of each package, hits the langchain 1.x ecosystem's internal version coupling, and backtracks through the entire release history trying to find a compatible set — effectively a hang.\n\nThis did not surface when I-carney-005 shipped Dockerfile.v6 because langchain 1.x had not been released / the resolver still converged then. Classic unbounded-dependency drift.\n\n## Acceptance criteria\n\n1. `requirements.txt:35-40` — add upper bounds to the langchain ecosystem pins. Pin to the **currently-working** versions: read what's actually installed in the working local env (`pip show langchain langchain-core langchain-openai langchain-community langgraph | grep -E 'Name|Version'`) and pin `>=<current>,<NEXT_MAJOR>` (e.g. `langchain>=0.3.27,<1.0.0`). The local env runs pipeline-A fine (the I-carney-006 smoke test passed), so its installed versions are the known-good floor.\n2. `docker compose -f docker-compose.v6.yml build` pip step converges in reasonable time (< 2 min for resolution) and the image builds.\n3. Verify pipeline-A still imports + runs after the pin tightening — `requirements.txt` is the pipeline-A surface, not just the Docker build, so the pins must not regress the sweep harness. Run a single-question smoke (`scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py --only clinical_tirzepatide_t2dm`) as the regression check.\n4. Codex diff review APPROVE.\n\n## Severity\n\nP0 for the Carney demo. `infra/vexxhost/provision.sh` runs `docker compose -f docker-compose.v6.yml up -d --build` — the sovereign deploy build would hang on the Vexxhost VM at the pip step. Blocks the deploy AND the G5 fallback-laptop drill.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Migrating pipeline-A to langchain 1.x — that's a deliberate framework upgrade with its own large test surface.  succeeded in 9089ms:
# I-perm-022 (#1214) — verifier span normalization: forensic plan (durable record)

Forensic by the parallel Claude agent (2026-06-11), persisted here so it is not lost.

## 1. Root cause (grounded in code + the real drb_76 run)

**The seam:** the cited span enters the four-role second evaluator via
`EvidenceDocument.text`, built in `src/polaris_graph/roles/native_gate_b_inputs.py`:
- `_resolve_evidence` (582-611) builds one `EvidenceDocument(doc_id=evidence_id,
  text=_cited_window_text(text, token))` per token (607-609).
- `_cited_window_text` (547-579) slices a bounded window but applies NO de-hyphenation /
  ligature / NFKD normalization — only `.strip()`.
- That `doc.text` is inlined verbatim as `{span}` into the Sentinel decomposition prompt
  (`sentinel_adapter.py:280-281`) and joined into the Judge `evidence_text`
  (`role_pipeline.py:333`). Both are LLM evaluators that read span TEXT.

A non-VERIFIED four-role verdict → `[confidence:low]` via `confidence_bucket`
(`generator/claim_labeler.py:29-53`), keyed on `is_verified` (four-role `final_verdict ==
"VERIFIED"`). This is the four-role seam, NOT a strict_verify Gemma drop.

**Mirror is NOT the target** — `mirror_adapter.py` binds on `doc_id`
(`_validate_citation_binding` 107-130), never on span text. Only the Sentinel
atom-by-atom span-coverage matcher and the Judge are artifact-sensitive.

## 2. HONEST empirical finding (the load-bearing correction)
From the real run `outputs/audits/I-perm-010/run_drb76_jun11/...`:
- The 58-row evidence pool DOES carry the artifacts: 12 ligature codepoints (U+FB01 `ﬁ`,
  U+FB02 `ﬂ`) + 5 line-break hyphens — ALL concentrated in ONE row `ev_206` (`bioﬁlms`,
  `inﬂammatory`, `dehy-\ndrogenase`, `pro-\nteins`, `oxaliplatin-\ninduced`).
- BUT `ev_206` is cited by exactly ONE claim (`04-000`), whose Sentinel verdict was
  SUPPORTED → ZERO of the 8 Sentinel-unsupported claims were ligature/hyphen-driven in this
  captured run.
- Most of the 8 Sentinel-unsupported claims are GENUINE semantic negatives ("span doesn't
  label the organism a pathogen"; "registry" vs "retrospective records"; "central-line" vs
  "indwelling catheters"). These MUST stay negative.
- ONE IS a real extraction-artifact surface mismatch: claim `05-007` — span "natural kill
  cell activity" vs claim "natural killer cell activity" (dropped "er" from PDF extraction).

**Honest root_cause:** un-normalized PDF-extraction artifacts (ligatures, line-break
hyphenation, truncation/whitespace) in the cited span reach the LLM evaluators verbatim and
can flip a genuinely-supported atom to "unsupported." Real, demonstrated CLASS of FN, but
"drb_76 = 12 FN" is NOT proven artifact-driven. **The fix must recover only true positives
the artifacts hid; it must NOT be tuned to "recover 12" (that would force-pass genuine
negatives).** Per-claim causal proof is the DoD paid §-1.1 smoke.

## 3. Design — normalize the SPAN only, grade the CLAIM as-authored
Artifacts are source-side. The generator's claim prose is clean; normalizing it would grade
a rewritten claim. So:
- SPAN: apply new `_normalize_span_text()` right before `EvidenceDocument(...)` (607-609).
- CLAIM: unchanged into the evaluator.
- Cache key only: normalize both for key identity (does not change what is evaluated).

`_normalize_span_text` — strictly meaning-preserving, ZERO digit modification:
1. Ligatures (the clean win): explicit map `ﬁ→fi ﬂ→fl ﬀ→ff ﬃ→ffi ﬄ→ffl ﬅ→ft ﬆ→st` OR NFKD
   restricted to the ligature range. Reuse `provenance_generator.py:195-238`
   (`_build_normalized_view` already does NFKD + ligature decomposition).
2. Line-break de-hyphenation, ALPHABETIC-ONLY: join `[a-z]-\n[a-z]`. Document the residual
   ambiguity (`oxaliplatin-\ninduced`→`oxaliplatininduced` cosmetically wrong;
   `dehy-\ndrogenase`→`dehydrogenase` right) — `TEXT_DEHYPHENATE` is a PyMuPDF
   extraction-time flag, unavailable at the seam. Restrict to alphabetic so digits untouched.
3. Whitespace: collapse runs, NBSP/zero-width → space.
4. NEVER touch digits or dashes between digits (I-gen-005 range/negative corruption lesson):
   every regex matches alphabetic context only.

Verdict cache (scope-limited under the 200-LOC cap): the in-run dedup at
`sweep_integration.py:283 _dedup_key` already keys on `(claim_text, sorted((doc_id, d.text)),
severity, s0_categories)`. Because we normalize UPSTREAM of `EvidenceDocument`, `d.text` is
already normalized → the existing dedup inherits the normalized `(claim, evidence_id, span)`
identity for free, no new cache code. A PERSISTENT cross-run verdict store is a larger build →
SPLIT to a follow-up issue.

## 4. Faithfulness (the critical argument)
Repair, not rewrite: maps a garbled token to its true form, adds NO content. If the span
genuinely doesn't state the atom (the 8 genuine negatives), the repaired span STILL doesn't
→ stays unsupported. Zero digit modification (testable invariant): `"reduced 2%"` and
`"reduced 20%"` can never collapse. Gate untouched (D8, `_compose_final_verdict` fail-closed,
Mirror doc_id binding, Sentinel UNGROUNDED override, VERIFIED coverage credit all unchanged).
No recovery target — accepts whatever recovers (possibly ≪12).

## 5. Edits (default-OFF flag `PG_GATE_B_SPAN_NORMALIZE`, default "0" → byte-identical)
- `src/polaris_graph/roles/native_gate_b_inputs.py`: add `_GATE_B_SPAN_NORMALIZE_ENV` +
  ligature map; add `_normalize_span_text(text)` (OFF-guarded → returns input unchanged);
  wrap the windowed text at `_resolve_evidence` (607-609):
  `text=_normalize_span_text(_cited_window_text(text, token))`. Records keep FULL
  un-normalized text (entity coverage matching unaffected — matches DOI/PMID/URL).
- Wire `PG_GATE_B_SPAN_NORMALIZE=1` into the Gate-B slate.

## 6. Tests (`tests/polaris_graph/test_span_normalize_iperm022.py`, mirror test_fx03)
- flag-off byte-identical; ligature repaired when on (real `ev_206` strings); line-break
  hyphen joined (alphabetic) + document `oxaliplatin-` residual; recovers a TP the artifact
  hid; **numeric difference does NOT collapse (adversarial: 2% vs 20% keys differ)**; genuine
  negative stays negative (repair adds no content).

## 6b. Honesty caveats
- "12 FN" NOT artifact-confirmed in the captured run; ligatures are the clean win, hyphenation
  best-effort/alphabetic-only/documented; truncation ("natural kill[er]") is NOT fixed by
  ligature/hyphen (needs fuzzy/edit-distance = different, riskier; out of scope). Persistent
  cross-run cache deferred. Paid §-1.1 smoke = the causal per-claim proof.

Key files: `native_gate_b_inputs.py` (547-611), `sentinel_adapter.py:280-281`,
`role_pipeline.py:333`, `mirror_adapter.py` (ruled out), `claim_labeler.py:29-53`,
`sweep_integration.py:283`, `provenance_generator.py:195-238`. Test pattern:
`tests/polaris_graph/test_fx03_gate_b_cited_span_iready017.py`.
